meta.title: India — The Four Labour Codes vs Legacy Acts
meta.subtitle: Consolidating 29 central labour laws into 4 Codes
meta.status_note: All four Codes received Presidential assent (Wages Code 2019; IR / SS / OSH Codes 2020). Implementation has been phased — the 'appointed day' under each Code's Section 1(3) activates once Central + State rules are notified. As of 2025-26, most States have pre-published draft rules; Centre has repeatedly signalled a synchronous roll-out. Employers should treat the new wage definition as the most imminent compliance change.

codes.name: The Code on Wages, 2019
codes.biggest_impact: Uniform 'wages' definition forces basic to be ≥ 50% of total remuneration — enlarges PF / gratuity / leave-encashment bases.

key_changes.old: Each statute defined wages separately. Employers typically set Basic + DA at 30–40% of CTC; HRA, special allowance, LTA, bonus kept large to minimise PF/gratuity liability.
key_changes.new: Single definition: 'wages' = basic pay + DA + retaining allowance. Specified exclusions (HRA, conveyance, OT, bonus, commission, employer PF, gratuity) are capped at 50% of total remuneration. If exclusions exceed 50%, the excess is ADDED BACK to wages.
key_changes.impact: PF & gratuity bases rise 30–60%. Employer statutory cost ↑ 4–12%. Take-home ↓ 3–8%. Biggest hit on mid-to-senior salaries where allowances dominated.

key_changes.old: No central floor. States set their own minimum wages under MW Act 1948, with wide variance.
key_changes.new: Centre fixes a national floor wage. State minimum wage cannot fall below it. Revised every 5 years (or sooner).
key_changes.impact: Low-wage states must raise rates. Contract-labour and gig workers benefit most.

key_changes.old: Only 'scheduled employments' notified by each government.
key_changes.new: Universal — applies to ALL employment, organised & unorganised. No scheduling required.
key_changes.impact: Previously-uncovered workers (domestic, agri, gig) now entitled.

key_changes.old: Factories Act: 2× ordinary wage rate.
key_changes.new: Same 2× rate retained, but calculated on the broader 'wages' definition above.
key_changes.impact: OT cost ↑ proportional to the new basic.

key_changes.old: Payment of Wages Act: 7th of next month (est ≤ 1000 workers) or 10th (larger).
key_changes.new: Daily: end of shift. Weekly: last day of week. Fortnightly: 2 days after end. Monthly: 7th of next month (all sizes).
key_changes.impact: Minor for most monthly payrolls.

key_changes.old: Payment of Bonus Act 1965: 8.33% min – 20% max on wages up to ₹21,000.
key_changes.new: Retained at 8.33–20% — but 'wages' now uses Code definition, and ceiling to be re-notified. Disqualifications under Sec 29 tightened (fraud, violent misconduct).
key_changes.impact: Bonus quantum up where wages ceiling is raised; clearer disqualification grounds.

key_changes.old: ER Act 1976: gender only.
key_changes.new: Covers all grounds of discrimination in recruitment, wages, conditions.
key_changes.impact: Broader anti-discrimination cover.

codes.name: The Code on Social Security, 2020
replaces: Employees' Provident Funds & Misc Provisions Act 1952
Building & Other Construction Workers Welfare Cess Act 1996
codes.biggest_impact: Gratuity eligibility on 1 year for fixed-term employees; gig / platform workers brought into social security net.

key_changes.old: Payment of Gratuity Act: 5 continuous years of service.
key_changes.new: 5 years continues for regular employees. Fixed-Term Employees: eligible after 1 year pro-rata. Working journalists: 3 years.
key_changes.impact: FTE contracts now carry gratuity from Year 1 — employer provision accrues earlier.

key_changes.old: 12% of Basic+DA; EPS ceiling ₹15,000 (EPF Scheme 1952 Para 26A).
key_changes.new: 12% of 'wages' (Code on Wages definition). EPS ceiling retained ₹15,000 pending notification. International Workers: pro-rata PF retained.
key_changes.impact: PF contributions rise where basic < 50% of CTC previously.

key_changes.old: Excluded from formal social security.
key_changes.new: Defined; social security schemes (life/disability cover, health, maternity, accident) funded via aggregator contribution 1–2% of annual turnover, capped at 5% of aggregate payment to workers.
key_changes.impact: Swiggy/Ola/Zomato-type platforms owe social security contributions.

key_changes.old: Applicable to notified establishments with ≥ 10 employees where gross ≤ ₹21,000/month.
key_changes.new: Central Govt may extend ESI to any establishment including hazardous units with <10 workers; all districts. Wage threshold retained ₹21,000 (₹25,000 for persons with disability).
key_changes.impact: More establishments drawn in; premiums unchanged.

key_changes.old: Maternity Benefit Act: 26 weeks for 1st two children; 12 weeks thereafter.
key_changes.new: Retained 26 / 12 weeks. Integrated with ESI framework.
key_changes.impact: No change in quantum; simpler enforcement.

key_changes.old: Employee's Compensation Act 1923 — schedule-based, often outdated.
key_changes.new: Retained but subsumed. Computation aligned with 'wages' def.
key_changes.impact: Compensation payouts rise with basic.

codes.name: The Industrial Relations Code, 2020
codes.biggest_impact: Layoff / retrenchment / closure threshold raised from 100 to 300 workers; 'Fixed-Term Employment' formalised with statutory benefits.

key_changes.old: Prior Govt permission needed for firms with ≥ 100 workers (ID Act Ch VB).
key_changes.new: Threshold raised to 300 workers. Below 300: notice + compensation only.
key_changes.impact: Large employers gain flexibility; political hot-button.

key_changes.old: Permitted by rules but without statutory parity of benefits.
key_changes.new: Codified: FTE gets SAME wages, benefits and service conditions as permanent employee, plus pro-rata gratuity after 1 year.
key_changes.impact: FTE cost approaches permanent cost; reduces mis-classification risk.

key_changes.old: Only 'public utility' establishments needed notice (14 days).
key_changes.new: 14-day notice mandatory in ALL establishments. Strike during pendency of conciliation/arbitration/tribunal → illegal.
key_changes.impact: Wildcat strikes curtailed.

key_changes.old: Applicable to establishments with ≥ 100 workers.
key_changes.new: Threshold raised to 300 workers. Model standing orders prescribed centrally.
key_changes.impact: Mid-size firms exempt; admin burden eases.

key_changes.old: Not a concept.
key_changes.new: Employer contributes 15 days' last-drawn wages per retrenched worker to a Re-skilling Fund disbursed to worker within 45 days.
key_changes.impact: Additional exit cost for retrenchment.

key_changes.old: No formal criterion — disputes on representation common.
key_changes.new: Sole negotiating union = union with ≥ 51% of workers as members. Otherwise, Negotiating Council of unions with ≥ 20% each.
key_changes.impact: Clear recognition process.

codes.name: The Occupational Safety, Health & Working Conditions Code, 2020
codes.biggest_impact: Daily work-hour cap at 12 (with weekly cap 48) — enables 4-day work week; universal appointment letter; one licence-one return-one registration regime.

key_changes.old: Factories Act: 9 hrs/day, 48 hrs/week. OT beyond 9.
key_changes.new: Max 12 hrs/day (incl rest + OT) retained BUT 48 hrs/week cap preserved — enabling 4-day × 12-hr week by notification. Spread-over 12 hrs.
key_changes.impact: Flex work-week legally possible. Employers may compress workweek if worker consents.

key_changes.old: Factories Act: OT 2× wage beyond 9 hrs/day or 48/week.
key_changes.new: 2× rate retained. OT cap raised — quarterly limit 125 hrs (Factories Act was 50).
key_changes.impact: More OT legally permissible.

key_changes.old: Factories Act: 1 day leave per 20 days worked (adult) / 15 (child). Earn leave only after 240 days continuous service in a calendar year.
key_changes.new: Entitlement after 180 days (down from 240). 1 day per 20 retained. Carry-forward cap 30 days; balance above encashable at year-end.
key_changes.impact: Earlier leave accrual; mandatory encashment protects worker.

key_changes.old: Not mandated by most acts; HR practice only.
key_changes.new: Statutory: every worker must receive an appointment letter.
key_changes.impact: Reduces informal employment; aids wage-protection enforcement.

key_changes.old: CLRA Act: ≥ 20 contract workers.
key_changes.new: Raised to ≥ 50 contract workers for licence.
key_changes.impact: Smaller contractors exempt from licencing.

key_changes.old: Inter-State Migrant Workmen Act 1979 limited in scope.
key_changes.new: Self-registration via portal; portability of PDS benefits; free annual to/from travel allowance by employer; Helpline.
key_changes.impact: Post-COVID response; employer helpline obligation.

key_changes.old: Factories Act: night-shift restrictions.
key_changes.new: Women permitted in all establishments for all shifts with consent + safety + transport.
key_changes.impact: 24×7 operations may employ women.

for_employer: Statutory cost ↑ 4–12% (PF, gratuity, bonus, leave-encashment all on broader wage base).
Fixed-Term Employment viable with full parity + 1-yr gratuity.
Compliance simpler: one registration, one licence, one return per code (vs 29 acts earlier).
for_employee: Take-home ↓ 3–8% in first year of implementation; retirement corpus grows.
Gratuity accessible to FTE after 1 year.
Universal minimum wage; universal appointment letter.
Gig / platform workers enter social-security net.
for_payroll_tech: Wage-definition engine must recompute basic = max(declared basic, 50% of total remuneration net of exclusions).
Two parallel runs during transition: old-act compliance until State appointed day, new-code compliance thereafter.
Audit trails must show both treatments for arrears.
